name: Lucid x Greater Boston STEM Summer Program Directories
brief: Find Massachusetts / Greater Boston online directories, parent guides, and editorial roundups that list student STEM summer programs, camps, and enrichment opportunities. Goal is to get Lucid Academy added to those listings. Need to reach the editor, list manager, or partnerships contact — the human who actually adds entries — not generic info@ inboxes.

offer.summary: 1:1 AI/ML research mentorship for high schoolers — students build original AI projects with ISEF/Regeneron mentors and place at competitions and university labs.
proof: 125+ high school alumni with shipped AI projects and national competition awards
Runs year-round and summer cohorts — clean fit for any 'summer STEM programs' listing
Remote-friendly so it serves Greater Boston families regardless of which town the directory targets
offer.icp_for_referral: Parents of MA high schoolers searching directories for a substantive summer STEM / AI / research program that goes beyond intro coding camp.

search.query: Massachusetts and Greater Boston parenting websites, local family magazines, school resource hubs, and enrichment directories that publish curated lists of summer STEM, coding, AI, or research programs for middle and high school students.
criteria: Website is a directory, guide, blog, or local publication — not a single program's marketing site
Audience is parents in Massachusetts, especially Greater Boston, MetroWest, Cambridge, or North Shore
Has at least one existing list/article of STEM, coding, AI, or summer programs for students
Accepts new listings, submissions, sponsored placements, or has an editor/partnerships contact
Actively maintained — at least one piece of content published in the last 12 months
Not a national aggregator with no MA-specific curation (e.g. skip generic Niche/CampPage-style mega-sites)
search.target_persona: Editor, List Manager, Partnerships Lead, or Founder

enrichments.label: editor_or_partnerships_contact
enrichments.description: Name + email of the editor, list manager, or partnerships lead; not a generic info@.

enrichments.label: existing_stem_list_url
enrichments.description: URL of their existing STEM / summer / coding programs list, if any.

enrichments.label: submission_process
enrichments.description: How new listings get added: form, email pitch, paid sponsorship, or editorial only.

enrichments.label: geo_focus
enrichments.description: Specific MA region the publication centers on (e.g. Boston, MetroWest, Cambridge).

enrichments.label: last_updated
enrichments.description: Approximate date of most recent post or list update on the site.

email_angles.angle: Fill a gap in your existing list
email_angles.hook: Saw your [year] roundup of Boston-area summer STEM programs — it's one of the few that actually curates instead of dumping every camp in the state.
email_angles.body_outline:
- Reference their specific list by name and note what's missing (1:1 AI research mentorship vs. group coding camps)
- Introduce Lucid in two lines: 125+ students, original AI projects, ISEF/Regeneron mentors, runs in summer
- Offer a ready-to-paste blurb + logo + one parent-facing testimonial so adding us is zero work
email_angles.cta: Want me to send the listing copy in the format you usually use?

email_angles.angle: Useful for your readers, not a sponsorship pitch
email_angles.hook: Quick note from the team at Lucid Academy — not a sponsorship pitch, just want to be useful to your readers.
email_angles.body_outline:
- Acknowledge they get pitched constantly and most are intro-level coding camps
- Lucid serves the higher end of the parent audience — kids who are past intro and want real research output
- Happy to write a short guest piece on 'how to evaluate a high school AI research program' if that's more useful than a listing
email_angles.cta: Listing, guest piece, or both — what's easiest on your side?

email_angles.hook: Reaching out about getting Lucid Academy added to your STEM programs directory.
email_angles.body_outline:
- Be direct: we're a 1:1 AI research mentorship program for high schoolers, remote, runs summer + year-round
- List proof in one line: 125+ alumni, ISEF/Regeneron mentor roster, competition and lab placements
- Mention we can do a referral / affiliate arrangement if their directory works that way, or just a free listing if it doesn't
email_angles.cta: What's the fastest path to getting added — a form, a paid slot, or just replying with our info?

qa_checklist: Editor replies asking for listing copy, logo, or a blurb
Lucid actually appears on their directory or roundup post within 2-4 weeks
Reply comes from a named human editor, not an info@ autoresponder
At least one publication offers a guest post or feature beyond a basic listing
Referral traffic shows up from MA-based directory domains in analytics
